core.action: Prioritize analyst action phrase matching (upgrade/downgrade detection) over generic keyword matching in the classification pipeline; analyst actions are high-signal events that must be correctly identified

consequence.description: Without proper priority handling, analyst upgrades/downgrades are misclassified as generic sentiment, causing high-signal catalyst events to be ignored or false catalysts to trigger incorrectly

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-021

validation_threshold: catalyst classification priority order defined AND analyst phrase matching evaluated before generic keyword matching → PASS; analyst matching missing OR equal/lower priority than generic keywords → FAIL

core.when: When implementing confidence-weighted valuation synthesis across multiple methods
core.action: Normalize confidence weights to sum to exactly 1.0 before synthesis; explicitly filter out methods with zero confidence before normalization to prevent division-by-zero or NaN propagation

consequence.description: Weights not summing to 1.0 cause systematic valuation bias where the final estimate is either scaled up or down incorrectly, leading to mispriced positions and incorrect allocation decisions

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-015

core.when: When implementing portfolio optimization or risk calculation requiring covariance matrices
core.action: Assume the framework handles covariance matrix positive semi-definite (PSD) repair — the framework does not implement PSD validation or repair; raw historical return data may produce non-PSD matrices due to estimation error

consequence.description: Non-PSD covariance matrices cause portfolio optimization to fail or produce mathematically invalid results, leading to incorrect portfolio weights and systematic risk mismeasurement in production

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-016

core.when: When computing covariance matrices from historical returns for portfolio optimization
core.action: Apply PSD repair before optimization — use Higham (2002) nearest PSD projection, eigenvalue clipping (set negative eigenvalues to epsilon), or spectral decomposition with rank-k approximation; validate with np.linalg.eigvalsh() ensuring each eigenvalues >= 1e-8

consequence.description: Without PSD repair, Cholesky decomposition fails in optimization routines, causing portfolio construction to crash or produce invalid allocations that misrepresent actual risk exposure

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-016

validation_threshold: eigenvalue_min >= 1e-8 → PASS; eigenvalue_min < 1e-8 AND no repair applied → FAIL

core.when: When implementing portfolio and trade persistence in database operations
core.action: Replace SQLAlchemy ORM models with raw SQL queries or direct SQL execution — the ORM abstraction layer must be maintained for each Portfolio and Trade persistence operations

consequence.description: Using raw SQL bypasses the ORM abstraction layer, introducing SQL injection vulnerabilities and breaking audit trail consistency; production systems lose the ability to track trade history and portfolio performance reliably

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-051

validation_threshold: Portfolio.create() or Trade.create() usage count == number of persistence operations → PASS

core.when: When configuring stock screener parameters for stock_selection stage
core.action: Verify default screener thresholds (price, volume, sector, market cap) match the target investment strategy — do not use default quantitative filters without explicit validation against strategy requirements; document any deviation from default parameters

consequence.description: Default screener criteria filter stocks based on generic thresholds, potentially excluding valid candidates for factor-based or sector-specific strategies, causing systematic underperformance in backtested results compared to live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-059

validation_threshold: screener_config != default_config AND documentation exists → PASS; screener_config == default_config AND no strategy_match_verification → WARN

core.when: When executing batch data fetching operations for large-scale historical data
core.action: Implement error handling with circuit-breaker pattern: track consecutive failures, halt batch processing after N failures (configurable threshold), require manual review before resuming; log each failed symbol with error reason for debugging

consequence.description: Sequential batch processing without circuit-breaker allows partial failures to cascade, resulting in incomplete datasets where missing symbols cause silent backtest errors or strategy decisions based on incomplete price history

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-060

core.when: When implementing or refactoring the investment analysis workflow
core.action: Use multi-agent architecture with parallel specialized analyst roles (fundamental, technical, sentiment, risk) and aggregate signals via consensus or voting mechanism

consequence.description: Replacing multi-agent architecture with a single agent removes domain-specific expertise and parallel analysis capabilities, causing less comprehensive market assessment and potentially missing critical signals

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-058

core.when: When implementing sensitivity analysis for strategy parameter tuning
core.action: Use uniform linspace sampling with 11-point grid within ±20% parameter range for sensitivity analysis

consequence.description: Using alternative sampling methods like Monte Carlo or Latin hypercube may violate the framework's expected grid structure and cause compatibility issues with visualization and interpretation modules

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-070

core.when: When using DBSCAN clustering for SEC filing document section detection
core.action: Verify DBSCAN epsilon and min_samples parameters are calibrated for 10-K/10-Q formatting conventions; for non-SEC financial documents, re-tune parameters before applying

consequence.description: Using DBSCAN with SEC-calibrated parameters on other financial documents causes inaccurate section detection, leading to incomplete or incorrect document parsing and potential loss of critical filing information

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-062

core.when: When calculating RSI for equity technical analysis
core.action: Use 14-period RSI with 70/30 overbought/oversold thresholds for equity ticker charts; for commodity or forex instruments, adjust period parameter accordingly

consequence.description: Using 14-period RSI on non-equity instruments produces misleading overbought/oversold signals due to different volatility characteristics, potentially causing incorrect trading signals

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-063

core.when: When calculating MACD for daily and weekly trend charts
core.action: Use 12/26/9 MACD parameters for daily and weekly equity charts; use 5/13/4 parameters for intraday charts to achieve faster response

consequence.description: Using standard 12/26/9 MACD on intraday charts produces delayed trend signals, while using 5/13/4 on daily/weekly charts introduces excessive noise and false crossover signals

evidence_refs.summary: 612a981 Derived from BD: BD-064

validation_threshold: macd_fast == 12 AND macd_slow == 26 AND macd_signal == 9 AND timeframe == 'intraday' → FAIL; macd_fast == 5 AND macd_slow == 13 AND macd_signal == 4 AND timeframe != 'intraday' → FAIL

core.when: When implementing or consuming the SEC filings API endpoint that returns multiple document sections
core.action: Verify client capability to parse multipart/mixed MIME responses before relying on bundled responses; implement fallback or alternative endpoint for clients that cannot handle multipart responses

consequence.description: Simple GET callers and basic HTTP clients cannot parse multipart/mixed responses, causing silent data retrieval failures where SEC filing sections are completely inaccessible

core.action: Verify that the API accepts JSON request body format for programmatic clients; if only Form defaults are supported, construct form-encoded requests or use the provided client SDK

consequence.description: RESTful JSON API clients expecting standard JSON request bodies will fail to upload files, causing data collection pipelines to break when switching from browser/curl testing to programmatic access
